title: Wholesome Turkish Lentil Soup
description: A cozy, grassy lentil soup with a glassy texture, red chilli flakes, and a fragrant tadka, perfect for an ayurvedic-rajasic friendly meal.
instructions: Rinse 200g red lentils until the water runs clear, then set aside.
In a large pot, heat 1 tbsp olive oil over medium heat and sauté the finely diced onion until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add 3 minced garlic cloves and 1 tbsp grated ginger, cooking for 1 minute while stirring.
Stir in 1 tsp ground cumin, 1 tsp ground coriander, and 1 tsp red chilli flakes, letting the spices bloom for 30 seconds.
Add 2 tbsp lemon juice, mixing well to combine.
Pour in 8 cups vegetable broth, bring the mixture to a boil.
Add the rinsed lentils, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 25 minutes, until lentils are tender and the soup thickens to a glassy consistency.
Season with 1 tsp salt to taste; remove the pot from heat.
Prepare a tadka by heating 1 tsp oil in a small pan, adding 1 tsp cumin seeds, letting them sizzle for 15 seconds, then pour the hot oil over the soup to release aromatic flavor.
Garnish with fresh cilantro before serving.
